feedback.text: I'm concerned with how games are nowadays moving digital only, with limited physical releases and ownage. I strongly support the goals of the Digital Fairness Act. I also believe it should protect consumers from losing access to video games they've legally purchased. I'll tell you a story: I bought one of the first versions of the Mass Effect trilogy (not the remaster) for 80EUR and already at the time of purchase one game (ME 1) was unplayable, non-refundable and non-functional. Later the second one (ME 2) and the company's technical support just tells you to buy the remaster. I already bought the game, I have paid for it here and I can't use it. This is unacceptable behavior for me and I would like to see some customer protection in this regard. Thank you.
